perturbation.original_text: e. "Net Revenue" shall mean gross receipts collected by Company or Reseller from the distribution of Company Products, [less third party commissions, equipment sales, delivery costs, taxes, refunds, and credits.
perturbation.changed_text: e. "Net Revenue" shall mean gross receipts collected by Company from the distribution of Company Products, without any deductions whatsoever, including but not limited to third party commissions, equipment sales, delivery costs, taxes, refunds, and credits.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Net Revenue" allows for deductions, whereas the modified definition specifies that no deductions are allowed. This creates a contradiction when calculating Referral Fees based on Net Revenue, because Exhibit B references the original definition of Net Revenue in Section 1e. This creates uncertainty in enforcement, specifically which definition of 'Net Revenue' should be used when calculating the commission, since both definitions are present in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESELLER COMPENSATION Earned Commission: Reseller will be compensated for Registered Referrals and Registered Opportunities (as defined in Section 1g, 1i.) based on the following schedule: Registered Referral: · 25% of Net Revenue (as defined in Section 1e.) with a COMPANY-RESELLER AGREED UPON SALE PRICE in writing, the case of COMPANY's software products. The Company's Telecom Services will be agreed upon on a case-by-case basis in writing. Registered Opportunity: · "Reseller" will purchase directly from the "Company" at a 25% discount off the COMPANY-RESELLER AGREED UPONsale price. · If sale is made through SEWP, there will be an additional 4% discount off the sale price to cover the Prime contractusage fee (typically 7-10%) off the COMPANY-RESELLAR AGREED UPON SALE PRICE.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Net Revenue" explicitly disallows deductions, directly conflicting with the contradicted text, which calculates Reseller compensation as a percentage of "Net Revenue (as defined in Section 1e.)". Since the original definition allowed for deductions but the revised definition does not, the contradiction introduces uncertainty in calculating Reseller compensation, as it is unclear whether the calculation should include or exclude deductions. This directly impacts the amount of compensation and renders the compensation clause ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Exclusivity on Registered Referrals and Opportunities. During the Term hereof and for a period of six (6) months following the termination of this Agreement or the discontinuation of any of the Company Products, (i) the Reseller shall have the exclusive right to commission for any Registered Referrals, (ii) the Company shall not market, promote, sell, or distribute Company Products or solicit or procure orders for the Company Products, or for any product(s) or service(s) similar to the Company Products, in the Territory other than through the Reseller and pursuant to this Agreement, except with the prior written consent of the Reseller, and (iii) without limitation to the foregoing, the Company shall not, directly or through other parties (whether agents, representatives, intermediaries, resellers or other parties), market, promote, sell, distribute, solicit or procure orders to any existing or prospective customer of the Reseller.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Exclusivity on Registered Referrals and Opportunities. During the Term hereof and for a period of six (6) months following the termination of this Agreement or the discontinuation of any of the Company Products, (i) the Reseller shall have the exclusive right to commission for any Registered Referrals, (ii) the Company shall not market, promote, sell, or distribute Company Products or solicit or procure orders for the Company Products, or for any competitive product(s) or service(s), in the Territory other than through the Reseller and pursuant to this Agreement, except with the prior written consent of the Reseller, and (iii) without limitation to the foregoing, the Company shall not, directly or through other parties (whether agents, representatives, intermediaries, resellers or other parties), market, promote, sell, distribute, solicit or procure orders to any existing or prospective customer of the Reseller.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the Company cannot market, promote, sell or distribute 'any product(s) or service(s) similar to the Company Products'. The changed text alters the phrase to 'any competitive product(s) or service(s)'. The terms 'similar' and 'competitive' are not synonymous, and are not defined, creating an ambiguity. This in-text contradiction affects Section 10, Termination, because a breach of section 5 can lead to termination, creating uncertainty in enforcement on what constitutes a breach due to what's considered competitive vs similar.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Termination. a. Unless terminated earlier as provided herein, this Agreement shall have the Term set forth above (which commences on the Effective Date). b. This Agreement may be terminated by Reseller for cause immediately upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (i) if the use or sale of Company Products are enjoined due to any claim of infringement of any U.S. patent or copyright or of any misappropriation of trade secrets; (ii) if Company ceases to do business, or otherwise terminates its business operations; or (iii) if Company breaches any provision of this Agreement and fails to fully cure such breach within fifteen (15) business days of written notice from Reseller describing the breach. c. This Agreement may be terminated by Company for cause immediately upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (i) if Reseller ceases to do business, or otherwise terminates its business operations; or (ii) if Reseller breaches any provision of this Agreement and fails to fully cure such breach within fifteen (15) business days of written notice from Company describing the breach. d. Neither party shall incur any liability whatsoever for any damage, loss or expenses of any kind suffered or incurred by the other party arising from or incident to any termination of this Agreement, which complies with the terms of the Agreement, whether or not the terminating party is aware of any such damage, loss or expenses. 3 Source: BRAVATEK SOLUTIONS, INC., 8-K, 4/18/2017 e. Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, other than with respect to customers of the Reseller existing at any time prior to the termination or expiration and with respect to ongoing sales or prospective sales, Reseller (i) shall immediately discontinue any use of the name, logotype, Marks or slogans of Company, (ii) shall immediately discontinue all representations or statements from which it might be inferred that any relationship exists between the parties, (iii) will cease to promote, solicit orders for or procure orders for the Product, (iv) will immediately return to Company all Proprietary Information (as defined below) and any other information or materials of Company in its possession, custody or control in whatever form held (including copies or embodiments thereof relating thereto). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the right to payments accruing prior to the termination or expiration shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the Company from marketing 'any product(s) or service(s) similar to the Company Products,' while the changed text narrows this restriction to 'any competitive product(s) or service(s)'. The explanation correctly points out that 'similar' and 'competitive' are not synonymous and undefined terms. This creates an ambiguity. Section 10 allows for termination if a party breaches the agreement and fails to cure the breach. Whether a breach has occurred now depends on interpreting 'competitive' versus 'similar', leading to uncertainty and potential disputes about whether a terminable breach has occurred. Therefore, the change creates a direct contradiction that could impact the enforceability of the termination clause in Section 10.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Liability Limitation. EXCEPT FOR IN THE EVENT OF WILLFUL MISCONDUCT OR GROSS NEGLIGENCE, THE RESELLER AND ITS AFFILIATES SHALL NOT BE LIABLE TO THE COMPANY, AND SHALL HAVE NO OBLIGATION TO INDEMNIFY OR HOLD HARMLESS THE COMPANY, WITH RESPECT TO OR IN CONNECTION WITH ANY LOSS RESULTING FROM OR CAUSED BY THE COMPANY PRODUCTS.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Liability Limitation. EXCEPT FOR IN THE EVENT OF WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, THE RESELLER AND ITS AFFILIATES SHALL NOT BE LIABLE TO THE COMPANY, AND SHALL HAVE NO OBLIGATION TO INDEMNIFY OR HOLD HARMLESS THE COMPANY, WITH RESPECT TO OR IN CONNECTION WITH ANY LOSS RESULTING FROM OR CAUSED BY THE COMPANY PRODUCTS.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause limits reseller liability except in cases of 'willful misconduct or gross negligence.' The modified clause removes 'gross negligence.' The term negligence is commonly understood. This now creates an in-text contradiction because the original clause implies potential liability for gross negligence, while the modified clause excludes that liability, potentially affecting Section 8, Warranty Disclaimer, which references liabilities between both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Warranty Disclaimer. RESELLER SHALL MAKE NO REPRESENTATION, GUARANTEE, OR WARRANTY CONCERNING THE COMPANY PRODUCT EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY AUTHORIZED IN ADVANCE BY COMPANY IN WRITING. TO THE EXTENT THE RESELLER HAS COMPLIED WITH THE OBLIGATIONS SET FORTH IN THIS SECTION, THE COMPANY SHALL INDEMNIFY AND HOLD HARMLESS THE RESELLER FOR ANY AND ALL LOSSES SUFFERED BY THE RESELLER OR ITS AFFILIATES (WHETHER DIRECTLY OR IN CONNECTION WITH A CLAIM BY A THIRD PARTY) AS A RESULT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE COMPANY PRODUCTS. ADDITIONALLY, THE RESELLER SHALL INDEMNIFY AND HOLD HARMLESS THE COMPANY FOR ANY AND ALL LOSSES SUFFERED BY THE COMPANY OR ITS AFFILIATES (WHETHER DIRECTLY OR IN CONNECTION WITH A CLAIM BY A THIRD PARTY) AS A RESULT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE RESELLER'S ACTIVITIES.
perturbation.justification: The modified clause in Section 12 removes 'gross negligence' from the liability limitation, meaning the reseller is now protected from liability for gross negligence where previously they were not. This directly contradicts Section 8, which states, 'ADDITIONALLY, THE RESELLER SHALL INDEMNIFY AND HOLD HARMLESS THE COMPANY FOR ANY AND ALL LOSSES SUFFERED BY THE COMPANY OR ITS AFFILIATES (WHETHER DIRECTLY OR IN CONNECTION WITH A CLAIM BY A THIRD PARTY) AS A RESULT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE RESELLER'S ACTIVITIES.' Since gross negligence is an activity of the reseller, the removal of gross negligence from Section 12 directly conflicts with the indemnification requirement imposed on the reseller in Section 8 for any and all losses, including those stemming from the reseller's activities. The contradiction is that the reseller's indemnification is broadened to include losses from 'gross negligence' in one area (Section 8) while in another (Section 12), the reseller is held to not be liable for the same activity. This contradiction creates an ambiguity and in-text contradiction, as it pertains to whether losses related to 'gross negligence' can be recovered from the reseller based on Section 8.
